TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT EXIST REGARDING DEFENDANT'S EFFORTS (OR LACK THEREOF) TO ADDRESS PLAINTIFF'S ISSUES WITH SMOKE AND NOISE. E.G. SEE FACTS 120, 116, 121. (THE SAME FACTS ARE REPEATED IN FOR THE SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND EACH SUMMARY ADJUDICATION REQUEST). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) AND CCP SECTION 437C(G). Off Calendar Nov-28-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Michelle L. Younkin; Saber Law Group; 415-278-1400; appearing on behalf of defendants Mercy Housing Management Group, Inc. and Edgar Perez.
Paul M. Collins; Tenant Law Group, PC; 888-510-7511; appearing on behalf of plaintiff Jon Hess.

Court reporter: Vanessa Clayton, CSR #13112, Vanessa.ClaytonCSR@hotmail.com, 209-662-2665.

Defendants contest the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MERCY HOUSING MANAGEMENT GROUP, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AGAINST PLAINTIFF JON HESS is DENIED. Triable issues of fact exist regarding defendant's efforts (or lack thereof) to address Plaintiff's issues with smoke and noise. E.g. See facts 120, 116, 121. (the same facts are repeated in for the summary judgment and each summary adjudication request).

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b) and CCP Section 437c(g). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Vanessa Clayton, CSR #13112, Vanessa.ClaytonCSR@hotmail.com, 209-662-2665. (D501)
